Just to clear up some misunderstandings here. The translator is doing an extremely bad job - “black face” in mandarin literally means someone who looks pissed off or upset. In this scenario, it’s referring to the guy having a “resting bastard face”.

In the past, changes in the weather drastically affected the fate and livelihood of humans. Terms like “under the weather” came as a result; to feel seasick or to be adversely affected by bad weather. “Black face” is no different - when there are thunderstorms, the sky turns dark or “black” as if the heavens are angry, upset or pissed off; therefore the term “black face” literally means someone who looks upset or pissed off.

Well, you can’t expect much when “Qi” gets translated to “Chi”

Just like we westerners associate colors with emotions (green with envy, yellow with lealousy, red with anger and love and so on) China and a lot of other east Asian countries associate black and white faces (sometimes badly translated as African and European) with different temperaments.
I believe it's stems from peasants working in the sun and their skin darkening , while aristocrats could stay in the shade and therefore had a lighter skin colour. Like the myth of aristocrats or royalty having "blue blood" in Europe

In Defense Of The Author:

They probably still teach things like art and literature to keep the culture of their old and destroyed country alive. 

  Men have always historically been the ones who go to war and protect a household. This is because they’re naturally stronger. In a time as despairing as this, a focus was probably made to be efficient as possible.
  
  The boys learn to fight and farm. The woman learn how to handle a household and about culture.  Could a woman learn the things the boys do and become a warrior? Obviously, but not at the mass produced level that men could.

  In the end I guess we have to face the fact that the equality, for the most part, we have today is a privilege of living in a relatively good era.

 If you disagree I would ,respectfully,love to hear why.

If you think sociologically, it makes sense for the values there to be completely different. That said, from another point of view, it's confusing how or why women and men would evolve or adapt in the same way they did on Earth. Perhaps, it's because, in that situation, the women need to carry children for nine months, meaning that they wouldn't require as much strength because they would be debilitated for months anyway. Meanwhile, the opposite holds true for men in that situation, as they need more strength to protect themselves and the mother of their offspring. This could create a cycle where women seek men who could protect them during pregnancy and perhaps promote polygamy. This would create a social value towards strength as the strength requirement for survival and dissemination of offspring would be higher. Honestly, I think a lot of those people's values couldn't be judged from our point of view (ethnocentrism), as they rightly should have wildly different values... That said, I'm almost certain that they would have different standards for beauty, with men preferring women with wider hips and more flesh and women preferring huge hulking men instead of handsome men.
